item.title: 在 VSCode 中安装 XDebug3 | Installing XDebug3 in VSCode
item.description:
发表于根据代码的情况调试代码，这通常是一种折磨，想象一下在不了解项目的情况下到达一个新环境，并且必须确定每个端点通过何处返回信息。

为了帮助这个过程，我们可以使用调试，在这篇文章中，我将展示如何安装 xdebug 并将它与xdebug一起使用。

安装 XDebug

首先要做的是安装 xdebug 如果您使用 linux，只需使用以下命令： 

 sudo apt-get install php7.4-xdebug

如果您使用不同版本的操作系统，请访问官网以获得更好的安装

激活 xdebug

下载并安装 XDebug 后，您必须在php.ini中注册并启用扩展。 为此，打开文件并在最后添加以下行：

 [ xdebug ] zend_extension = xdebug xdebug . mode = debug

现在一切都准备好了，我们只需要向 vscode 展示如何使用 xdebug，为此我们已经准备好了一个扩展，只需安装它

安装后，您可以单击侧边栏的"Run and Debug" ，当您按下启动时，配置文件将在.vscode/launch.json中自动创建

如果您遇到问题，这是文件：

在这个文件中，我们有两个设置供您在使用浏览器时使用，第二个是直接执行文件（就像使用命令行一样）

附加功能

码头工人

要在 docker 中使用，您需要遵循更多的点，您将遵循相同的过程，但是当您在 php.ini 中配置文件时，您需要再添加两行：

 xdebug.start_with_request = yes xdebug.client_host = host.docker.internal

要获得更完整的版本，请遵循 makefile：

最后修改您的launch.json ，只需添加和更改pathmappings

 "configurations" : [{ ..., "pathMappings" : { "remote/path/to/webroot/" : " ${ workspaceFolder } " , } }]

例子：

谢谢阅读！

如果您有任何问题、投诉或提示，您可以将它们留在评论中。 我很乐意回答！

支持我

迈克尔·安德鲁扎—— 一旦暂停，walternascimentobarroso 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，walternascimentobarroso 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，walternascimentobarroso 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 walternascimentobarroso 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 walternascimentobarroso 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 walternascimentobarroso 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Walter Nascimento

Posted on Aug 17

Debug the code depending on how the code is, it's often torture, imagine arriving in a new environment without knowing anything about the project and having to identify where each endpoint passes to return the information.

To help in this process we can use debug, for this post I will show how to install xdebug and use it together with vscode.

Installing XDebug

The first thing to do is install xdebug if you use linux just use the command:

sudo apt-get install php7.4-xdebug

💡Change php to the version you want

if you use a different version of operating system, visit the official website for a better installation

https://xdebug.org/docs/install

Activating xdebug

After XDebug is downloaded and installed, you'll have to register and enable the extension in your php.ini. For this, open the file and at the end add the following lines:

[xdebug]
zend_extension=xdebug
xdebug.mode=debug

💡 Remember to restart your web server after applying these changes.

VSCode

Now that everything is ready, we just need to show vscode how to use xdebug, and for that we have an extension ready, just install it

https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=xdebug.php-debug

VSCode config

After installation you can click on the sidebar on "Run and Debug" and when you press start the configuration file will be created automatically in .vscode/launch.json

If you're having trouble, this is the file:

in this file we have two settings one for you to use when using the browser and the second one is to directly execute a file (as if using the command line)

Extras

Docker

To use in docker you need to follow a few more points you will follow the same process, but when you configure the file in php.ini, you need to add two more lines:

xdebug.start_with_request=yes
xdebug.client_host=host.docker.internal

For a more complete version follow a makefile:

and finally modify your launch.json, just add and change pathmappings

"configurations": [{ ..., "pathMappings": { "remote/path/to/webroot/": "${workspaceFolder}", }
}]

Example:

Thanks for reading!

If you have any questions, complaints or tips, you can leave them here in the comments. I will be happy to answer!

😊😊 See you! 😊😊

Support Me

Youtube - WalterNascimentoBarroso
Github - WalterNascimentoBarroso
Codepen - WalterNascimentoBarroso

Mehedi Hasan Sagor - Jun 27

Michael Andreuzza - Jun 27

item.title: 关于静态网站的十大误区 | Ten Myths about Static Websites
item.description:
如果您曾经搜索过静态网站和动态网站之间的区别，您可能会发现一些关于静态网站可以做什么和不可以做什么的可疑陈述（如果不是完全错误的话）。

这些神话是来自误解还是误解，让我们澄清一下，好吗？

误区 1：静态网站很难编辑。

这个神话可能是我遇到的最常见的神话。 我猜它要么来自查看原始 Markdown 文件的人，但没有意识到编辑可以多么简单，要么来自静态站点页面必须逐个编辑的公认智慧（参见下面的神话 #2）。

事实是，有数十种具有静态功能的内容管理系统 (CMS)，它们使编辑静态网站就像在富文本编辑器中工作一样简单。 有些，比如 CloudCannon，甚至添加了高级可视化页面构建，因此任何人都可以编辑和创建静态页面，以及管理自己网站的内容。

误区 #2：您必须分别编辑每个静态页面。

好的，这是错误的，但这种误解是基于历史的。 在 1990 年代，您必须在网站的每个页面上复制布局、导航或页脚。

然而，这些天——我涵盖了过去二十年左右的时间——称为静态站点生成器 (SSG) 的工具使用模板来构建网站的所有页面。 您可以设计一次布局，然后将其应用于任意数量的页面。

误区 3：静态网站中没有 JavaScript。

老实说，我不确定这个是从哪里来的，或者一定是复杂的传闻链赋予了它生命。

一些 SSG 在构建网站时不使用 JavaScript； 其他人这样做，但尽量减少加载到客户端浏览器中的 JavaScript 数量。 然而，所有 SSG 共享的是，如果开发人员想要使用 JavaScript，则可以在网页中使用它。

误区四：静态网站不能使用外部数据或 API

静态站点通常使用外部数据存储，例如 JSON、CSV、XML、YML 和 TOML。 他们还将 API 用于分析、评论、搜索和电子商务等附加功能。

误区 5：静态意味着我不能使用视频或动画。

这可能是基于对“静态”一词的字面解释，意思是不动。 让记录显示静态站点仅在其交付方式上是静态的——该站点由 SSG 构建，然后立即准备好供用户浏览器加载，完整的 CSS 动画和尽可能多的嵌入视频，和猫的 GIF 动图，随心所欲地演奏键盘。

误区 6：静态只适用于小型网站。

一点也不。 这可能与神话 #2 有关，但我猜这与“构建时间”有关，即 SSG 构建 HTML 页面所需的时间。 事实上，像 Hugo 和 Eleventy 这样的 SSG 在构建大型站点方面的速度非常快。 像 CloudCannon 这样的 CMS（作为用户和技术含量更高的 SSG 之间的桥梁的内容管理层）也有助于减少非技术用户的构建时间，他们可以随意编辑并在选择时构建网站这样做。

误区 7：静态网站通常是在记事本等简单的文本编辑器中创建的。

不是理所当然的。 静态网站通常以两种主要方式创建，具体取决于制作者。 非技术编辑（如营销人员或文案）可以从模板创建新的静态站点，并通过用户友好的 CMS在浏览器中进行编辑。 开发人员通常会使用 CLI 工具在本地创建他们的站点，然后将这些文件连接到托管服务（并且可能是可视化 CMS，当他们将新站点移交给他们的内容或营销团队以供他们持续使用时）。

误区 8：网站的静态方法是“过时的”或“简单的”。

同样，这种误解植根于网络历史（静态交付是网络过去的工作方式），并带有不必要的负面含义。 静态交付很简单，因为它需要更少的额外服务、更少的计算、更少的能量，并且在其他条件相同的情况下，网页加载速度更快。 如果那是“过时的”，那就给我上色过时。 然而，这种方法不是过时的。 事实上，我什至建议静态站点——与它们的动态等价物相比，相互依赖的系统堆栈要小得多——将有一条更容易适应未来网络基础设施变化的途径。

需要明确的是，静态和动态站点总会有一个用例。 但人们似乎对这种评论的建议是，Web 开发人员应该始终使用最新最闪亮的工具，因为它们更新。 这很好，但前提是网站需要它们！ 事实是，使用静态方法可以更好地为许多网站提供服务（更快、更安全）。

误区 9：静态网站“不响应用户操作”。

公平地说，这个神话有点模糊，但它是唯一具有真理核心的神话。 静态站点对用户操作的响应非常好——页面加载速度非常快，并且与 API 和 JavaScript 一起为各种用例提供​​了出色的用户体验。

然而，静态网站不会根据用户的观看习惯或用户过去在网站上的操作向不同用户显示不同的内容。

误区 #10：“一旦构建了基本结构，就很难扩展静态网站以添加新内容。”

至于扩展您的网站以满足大量增加的用户需求，这更多地与您托管网站的方式有关，而不是“静态与动态”的问题。 无论您采用哪种方法，您都可以从范围广泛的主机和内容交付网络中进行选择。

就用新内容扩展静态站点而言，这个神话离事实太远了——就好像（在这里随意想象我最好的Don LaFontaine印象）我们生活在一个没有内容管理系统的世界里。 大多数 Web 开发人员都知道，构建网站的结构和范围功能可能是一项重大任务。 但是，一旦完成，将新内容添加到静态站点就像单击 CMS 中的按钮一样简单。

正如我上面提到的，CloudCannon 的存在是为了让每个人都可以更轻松地在静态站点上创建和编辑内容。 我们构建 CMS 的目的是消除或最小化静态网站的实际或感知缺陷，同时增强静态交付的许多好处。

我们有它。

嗯，就是这样。 我能找到的关于静态网站的最常见的神话，都以最少的麻烦和麻烦来解决。 如果你听说过任何其他关于静电的高大上的故事，我很想听听它们——让我知道！



原文:

If you’ve ever searched out the differences between static websites and dynamic websites, you’re likely to have discovered several questionable — if not outright false — statements about what static websites are and aren’t capable of.

Whether these myths came about from misunderstanding or misinterpretation, let’s set the record straight, shall we?

Myth #1: Static sites are difficult to edit.

This myth is probably the most common one I came across. I’m guessing it comes either from someone looking at a raw Markdown file, not realizing how simple it can be to edit, or the received wisdom that static site pages have to be edited one by one (see Myth #2 below).

The fact is, there are dozens of static-capable content management systems (CMSs) that make editing static websites as easy as working in a rich text editor. Some, like CloudCannon, even add advanced visual page-building into the mix, so anyone can edit and create static pages, as well as manage the contents of their own website.

Myth #2: You have to edit each static page separately.

Ok, this one’s false, but the misperception is grounded in history. In the 1990s you would have had to duplicate a layout, navigation, or footer across every single one of your site’s pages.

These days, however — and by that I encompass the last twenty years or so — tools called static site generators (SSGs) use templates to build all of a website’s pages. You can design a layout once, and it’s applied to as many pages as you want.

Myth #3: There’s no JavaScript in static sites.

I’m honestly not sure where this one comes from, or the complicated chain of hearsay that must have given it life.

Some SSGs do not use JavaScript when they build websites; others do, but try to minimize the amount of JavaScript that loads in the client’s browser. What all SSGs share, however, is the capacity to use JavaScript within a webpage if a developer wants to use it.

Myth #4: Static sites can’t use external data or APIs

Static sites commonly use external data stores such as JSON, CSV, XML, YML and TOML. They also use APIs for additional functions such as analytics, commenting, search, and ecommerce.

Myth #5: Static means I can’t use videos or animations.

This one’s probably based on a literal interpretation of the word ‘static’, meaning unmoving. Let the record show that a static site is static in its delivery method only — the site is built by the SSG, and is then immediately ready for user browsers to load, complete with the full spectrum of CSS animations, and as many embedded videos, and GIFs of cats playing keytars as you’d like.

Myth #6: Static is only good for tiny sites.

Not at all. This could be related to Myth #2, but I’m guessing this one is connected to the ‘build time’, which is how long the SSG takes to construct HTML pages. The truth is, SSGs like Hugo and Eleventy are incredibly fast at building even large sites. A CMS like CloudCannon — the content management layer that acts as a bridge between the user and the more technical SSG — also helps to reduce build time for non-technical users, who are able to edit as they please and build their sites when they choose to do so.

Myth #7: Static websites are usually created in simple text editors like Notepad.

Not as a matter of course. (Unless you really love Notepad.)

Static sites are usually created in two main ways, depending on who’s making them. Non-technical editors (like marketers or copywriters) can create new static sites from a template, and edit them in the browser via a user-friendly CMS. Developers will usually use CLI tools to create their sites locally, and then connect those files to a hosting service (and likely a visual CMS, when they hand off the new site to their content or marketing teams for their ongoing use).

Myth #8: The static approach for websites is “old-fashioned” or “simple”.

Again, this misconception is rooted in web history (static delivery is how the web used to work), and carries with it an unnecessary negative connotation. Static delivery is simple, in the sense that it requires fewer additional services, less computation, less energy, and all else being equal, the webpages load faster. If that’s “old-fashioned”, then color me old-fashioned. What this approach is not, however, is obsolete. In fact, I’d go so far as to suggest that static sites — which have a much smaller stack of interdependent systems than their dynamic equivalents — will have an easier path to adapt to future shifts in web infrastructure.

To be clear, there will always be a use case for both static and dynamic sites. But what people seem to be suggesting with this kind of comment is that web developers should always use the latest and shiniest tools because they’re newer. And that’s great, but only if the site needs them! The truth is, many websites would be better served (faster, more secure) with a static approach.

Myth #9: Static sites “don’t respond to user actions”.

This myth is a little vague, to be fair, but is the only one that has a kernel of truth. Static sites respond very well to user actions — the pages load incredibly quickly, and together with APIs and JavaScript, offer an excellent user experience for a wide range of use cases.

What static sites don’t do, however, is show different content to different users, based on their viewing habits or a user’s past actions on the site.

Myth #10: “It can be hard to scale a static website to add new content, once you’ve built the basic structure.”

As far as scaling your website for massively increased user demand, that’s more to do with how you host your site, rather than a question of ‘static versus dynamic’. Whichever approach you take, you can choose from a wide range of hosts and content delivery networks.

In terms of scaling up a static site with new content, this myth couldn’t be further from the truth — as though (and feel free to imagine my best Don LaFontaine impression here) we lived in a world without content management systems. CMSs solved the problem or adding, editing, and managing pages, assets, and site content many years ago, and we’ve been improving on these processes for a long time.

Most web developers will know that building out the structure and scoping the functionality of a website can be a significant task. Once that’s done, however, adding new content to a static site is as easy as clicking a button within a CMS.

As I’ve mentioned above, CloudCannon exists to make it easier for everyone to create and edit content on static sites. We’ve built our CMS around eliminating or minimizing the real or perceived drawbacks of static sites, all the while augmenting the many benefits of static delivery.

There we have it.

Well, that’s it. The most common myths about static websites I could find, all put to rest with a minimum of fuss and bother. If you’ve heard any other tall tales about static, I’d love to hear them — let me know!

item.title: HTML 块级和内联级元素权威指南 | The Definitive Guide to HTML Block Level and Inline Level Elements
item.description:
塞托夸梅

发表于在您作为 Web 开发人员的旅程中，要使 Web 开发顺利进行，您需要了解的一件事是 HTML 中的块级和内联级元素的概念。 了解这些元素和属性将帮助您轻松设置 HTML 样式。

块级元素从新行开始并占据页面的整个宽度，默认情况下，浏览器会在元素前后自动添加一些空间（边距）。 一些块级元素包括： 

 <div></div> <hr> <li></li> <ul></ul>

默认情况下，块级元素设置为块显示，并且能够“破解”它们的默认设置并操纵它们的行为，必须将显示属性的值重新分配给内联、弹性或它们的任何值会更喜欢。

内联级元素不会从新行开始，而是在它们周围流动文本，并且只占用必要的宽度。 以下是一些内联级别元素：

 <a></a> <button></button> <img>

为了能够更改内联级别元素的默认行为，必须将其显示属性的值更改为块或任何所需的值。

这里的所有都是它的。 这里没有什么复杂的。 因此，下次您尝试定位或对齐没有响应的元素时，请检查它是什么类型的元素，并让这篇简短的文章成为您的指南。

深夜编码器 - 一旦暂停，setorkwame 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，setorkwame 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，setorkwame 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 setorkwame 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 setorkwame 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 setorkwame 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Setor Kwame

Posted on Aug 17

In your journey as a web developer, one thing you will need understanding of to make web development smooth is the concept of block level and inline level elements in your HTML. An understanding of these elements and the properties will aid you in styling your HTML with little to no hassle.

HTML element types can be divided into two categories - block level elements and inline level elements.

Block level elements start on a new line and take up the entire width of a page and by default the browser automatically adds some space (a margin) before and after the element. Some block level elements include:

<div></div>
<hr>
<li></li>
<ul></ul>

Block level elements by default are set to a display of block and to be able to 'hack' into their default setting and manipulate their behavior, one will have to reassign the value of the display property to an inline, or flex or whatever value they will prefer.

Inline level elements do not start on a new line but instead flow with text around them and only takes up as much width as necessary. Here are some in-line level elements:

<a></a>
<button></button>
<img>

To be able to change the default behavior of an in-line level element, one will have to change the value of its, display property to a block or any desired value.

That's all there is to it. Nothing complex here. So the next time you are trying to position or align an element that isn't responding, check what type of element it is and let this short article be your guide.

Late Night Coder - Aug 15

item.title: 使用 FIFO 队列进行多处理通信 | Multiprocessing communication with FIFO queues
item.description:
马塞尔克鲁兹

发表于这是今天讨论 FIFO 和 IPC（进程间通信）技术的系列文章的一部分进程和子进程或子进程之间，通常这是在语言的标准库或第三方库中解决的，例如 node 的exec和spawn使用与我们将要使用的非常相似的东西为您处理进程之间的通信做，但是它是如何工作的，如果我们想要对它有更多的控制，我们可以做些什么，让我们探索一些选项。

没有错误处理的简单队列产生多个进程

编码

我总是喜欢从代码开始，因为阅读代码会让你对发生的事情有一个大概的了解，下面是非常简单的 ruby​​ 代码，它是用 Ruby 编写的，但是这个想法可以用在任何语言中，重点是想法不是实现。

上面是运行程序的终端，下面是我将 url 推送到 fifo 文件的另一个终端，以下是我尝试更详细地绘制正在发生的事情

使用错误处理生成多个进程的简单队列

我们上次实现中缺少的一件事是错误处理，如果在尝试发出 GET 请求的过程中出现问题，则消息丢失，该过程将崩溃或永远挂起，我们不会再试一次或得到任何回应。

我们希望提高弹性或我们的应用程序并使其更具容错性。

在这种情况下，我们可以做的是创建另一个队列来处理错误，也称为死信队列，每当我们在处理消息时出现错误，我们都会将消息推送到死信队列以供稍后处理。

编码

为此，我们只需要将错误推送到死信队列并处理死信，添加另一个进程只是为了处理死信队列，两个进程，处理输入队列的主进程和负责死信队列的进程同时运行并等待消息到达队列。

 require 'uri' require 'net/http' require 'json' OUTPUT_FIFO = './output.fifo' INPUT_FIFO = './input.fifo' DEADLETTER_FIFO = './deadletter.fifo' unless File . exist? ( OUTPUT_FIFO ) File . mkfifo ( OUTPUT_FIFO ) File . chmod ( 0666 , OUTPUT_FIFO ) end unless File . exist? ( INPUT_FIFO ) File . mkfifo ( INPUT_FIFO ) File . chmod ( 0666 , INPUT_FIFO ) end unless File . exist? ( DEADLETTER_FIFO ) File . mkfifo ( DEADLETTER_FIFO ) File . chmod ( 0666 , DEADLETTER_FIFO ) end def read_from_fifo ( fifo ) rpipe = File . open ( fifo , 'r+' ) res = "" while ( r = rpipe . read ( 1 )) != " 
 " res << r end res end def push_to_fifo ( endpoint , fifo ) wpipe = File . open ( fifo , 'w+' ) wpipe . write " #{ endpoint } 
 " wpipe . flush end def process_deadletter () puts "Waiting for messages in the dead letter queue" rpipe = File . open ( DEADLETTER_FIFO , 'r+' ) url = "" while ( r = rpipe . read ( 1 )) != " 
 " url << r end fork do begin puts "Dead Letter Processing: #{ url } PID: #{ Process . pid } " uri = URI ( url ) res = Net :: HTTP . get_response ( uri ) puts JSON . parse ( res . body )[ 'quote' ] push_to_fifo ( JSON . parse ( res . body )[ 'quote' ], OUTPUT_FIFO ) rescue => e puts "error: #{ e } " end end process_deadletter end def process_queue () puts "Waiting for messages in the process queue" rpipe = File . open ( INPUT_FIFO , 'r+' ) url = "" while ( r = rpipe . read ( 1 )) != " 
 " url << r end fork do puts "Processing: #{ url } PID: #{ Process . pid } " begin uri = URI ( url ) res = Net :: HTTP . get_response ( uri ) puts JSON . parse ( res . body )[ 'quote' ] push_to_fifo ( JSON . parse ( res . body )[ 'quote' ], OUTPUT_FIFO ) rescue => e puts "Error trying to process #{ url } sending message to deadletter queue" push_to_fifo ( url , DEADLETTER_FIFO ) end end process_queue end fork do process_deadletter end process_queue

如果我们运行前面的代码，我们会得到以下响应

现在我们有两个进程在队列中等待消息，如果我们将消息附加到 input.fifo 文件中

 echo 'https://api.kanye.rest/' >> input.fifo

我们得到回应

这里没有什么新东西，我们传递了一个有效的 url，一切正常，但是如果我们传递了一个无效的 URL，我们可以看到死信队列正在工作

echo 'invalid url' >> input.fifo

第一个进程失败并将消息发送到死信队列，死信队列进程开始处理队列中的新消息，它也失败了，因为在这种情况下它是一个无效的 URL

为第二次通话添加延迟

在实际情况下，我们希望为第二次调用添加延迟，如果我们正在访问的服务现在不工作，它不太可能在几毫秒后工作，在这种情况下我们想要做的是为了给处理死信队列的进程添加延迟，我们有很多不同的方法可以做到这一点，我们可以增加每次重试的重试时间，我们可以过滤掉一些错误，比如无效的 URL 情况，因为在这种情况下它永远不会起作用。

下一篇文章

在下一篇文章中，我将尝试其他设置队列和处理消息的方法。

一旦暂停，____marcell 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，____marcell 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，____marcell 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果____marcell 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记____marcell：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ____marcell 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Marcell Cruz

Posted on Aug 17

This is part of a series of posts talking about FIFO and techniques for IPC(inter process comunication) today I'm gonna explore IPC between parent and child processes for asynchronous programming, one problem that arises when you fork a process is communication between the parent process and the child process or between child processes, usually this is solved in the standard library of the language or a third party library, node's exec and spawn for example handles communication between the process for you using something very similar with what we're gonna do, But how does that work and what can we do if we want to have more control over it, Let's explore some options.

Simple Queue Spawning Multiple Processes Without Error handling

The code

I always like to start with the code, because reading the code will give you a general idea of what's going on, the following is very simple ruby code, it's written in Ruby but the idea can be used in any language, and the point is the idea not the implementation.

read_from_fifo reads from the fifo file line by line, push_to_fifo writes a line to the fifo file and process_queue reads from the input file forks the process passes the url that was read from the fifo file to the child process, the child process makes a http request and writes the result to an output fifo file, since process_queue is a recursive function that runs forever, any new line added to the fifo file will be processed.

require 'uri'
require 'net/http'
require 'json' OUTPUT_FIFO = './output.fifo'
INPUT_FIFO = './input.fifo' unless File.exist?(OUTPUT_FIFO) File.mkfifo(OUTPUT_FIFO) File.chmod(0666, OUTPUT_FIFO)
end unless File.exist?(INPUT_FIFO) File.mkfifo(INPUT_FIFO) File.chmod(0666, INPUT_FIFO)
end def read_from_fifo(fifo) rpipe = File.open(fifo, 'r+') res = "" while (r = rpipe.read(1)) != "
" res << r end res
end def push_to_fifo(endpoint, fifo) wpipe = File.open(fifo, 'w+') wpipe.write "#{endpoint}
" wpipe.flush
end def process_queue() rpipe = File.open(INPUT_FIFO, 'r+') url = "" while (r = rpipe.read(1)) != "
" url << r end fork do puts "Processing: #{url} PID: #{Process.pid}" uri = URI(url) res = Net::HTTP.get_response(uri) puts JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'] push_to_fifo(JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'], OUTPUT_FIFO) end process_queue
end push_to_fifo('https://api.kanye.rest/', INPUT_FIFO)
push_to_fifo('https://api.kanye.rest/', INPUT_FIFO)
push_to_fifo('https://api.kanye.rest/', INPUT_FIFO) process_queue()

Above is a terminal running the program, and below is another terminal where I pushed the url to the fifo file, the following is my attempt at drawing what is happening in more detail

Simple Queue Spawning Multiple Processes With Error Handling

One thing that was missing from our last implementation was error handling, if something goes wrong in the process that tries to make the GET request, the message is lost, the process is going to crash or hang forever we're not gonna try again or get any response.

We want to improve the resilience or our application and make it more fault tolerant.

What we can do in this situation is create another queue to process errors, also called a dead letter queue, any time we have a error processing a message we push the message to the dead letter queue to be processed later.

The Code

To do that we just need to push the error to the dead letter queue and to also process the dead letter, to do add we add another process just to process the dead letter queue, both processes, the main that processes the input queue and the process responsible for the dead letter queue run at the same time and wait for messages to get to the queue.

require 'uri'
require 'net/http'
require 'json' OUTPUT_FIFO = './output.fifo'
INPUT_FIFO = './input.fifo' DEADLETTER_FIFO = './deadletter.fifo' unless File.exist?(OUTPUT_FIFO) File.mkfifo(OUTPUT_FIFO) File.chmod(0666, OUTPUT_FIFO)
end unless File.exist?(INPUT_FIFO) File.mkfifo(INPUT_FIFO) File.chmod(0666, INPUT_FIFO)
end unless File.exist?(DEADLETTER_FIFO) File.mkfifo(DEADLETTER_FIFO) File.chmod(0666, DEADLETTER_FIFO)
end def read_from_fifo(fifo) rpipe = File.open(fifo, 'r+') res = "" while (r = rpipe.read(1)) != "
" res << r end res
end def push_to_fifo(endpoint, fifo) wpipe = File.open(fifo, 'w+') wpipe.write "#{endpoint}
" wpipe.flush
end def process_deadletter() puts "Waiting for messages in the dead letter queue" rpipe = File.open(DEADLETTER_FIFO, 'r+') url = "" while (r = rpipe.read(1)) != "
" url << r end fork do begin puts "Dead Letter Processing: #{url} PID: #{Process.pid}" uri = URI(url) res = Net::HTTP.get_response(uri) puts JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'] push_to_fifo(JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'], OUTPUT_FIFO) rescue => e puts "error: #{e}" end end process_deadletter
end def process_queue() puts "Waiting for messages in the process queue" rpipe = File.open(INPUT_FIFO, 'r+') url = "" while (r = rpipe.read(1)) != "
" url << r end fork do puts "Processing: #{url} PID: #{Process.pid}" begin uri = URI(url) res = Net::HTTP.get_response(uri) puts JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'] push_to_fifo(JSON.parse(res.body)['quote'], OUTPUT_FIFO) rescue => e puts "Error trying to process #{url} sending message to deadletter queue" push_to_fifo(url, DEADLETTER_FIFO) end end process_queue
end fork do process_deadletter
end process_queue

If we run the previous code we get the following response

Now we have two processes waiting for messages in the queue, if we append a message to the input.fifo file with

echo 'https://api.kanye.rest/' >> input.fifo

We get the response

Nothing new here, we pass a valid url and everything worked fine, but if we pass a invalid URL we can see the dead letter queue working

echo 'invalid url' >> input.fifo

The first process fails and send the message to the dead letter queue, the dead letter queue process starts processing the new message in the queue, it also fails since in this case it's a invalid URL

Adding A Delay To The Second Call

In a real situation we would want to add a delay to the second call, if the service that we're hitting is not working now, it's unlikely that it's going to be working some milliseconds later, what we want to do in this case is to add a delay to the process that handles the dead letter queue, we have a lot of different approaches to do that, we can increase the time we do the retry for every retry, we can filter out some errors like the invalid URL case, since it's never going to work in this case.

Next post

In the next post I'm gonna experiment with other ways of setting up the queues and processing the messages.

Bishwas Bhandari - Jul 13

Report other inappropriate conduct

item.author: ____marcell

item.title: 如何使用 Gravity AI 和 Meadowrun 部署 ML 模型 | How to Deploy ML Models Using Gravity AI and Meadowrun
item.description:
在本文中，我们将讨论部署这些容器的不同选项，然后通过Meadowrun将它们用于批处理作业，Meadowrun 是一个用于在云中运行 Python 代码和容器的开源库。

容器与库

本节列出了这篇文章的一些动机——如果你有兴趣让事情正常运行，请转到下一节！

如果您将 ML 模型视为一个库，那么将其发布为一个包似乎更自然，无论是在PyPI上与 pip 一起使用，还是在Anaconda.org上与 conda 一起使用，而不是容器。 这种方法有一些缺点：

  • 特别是对于 pip（不是 conda），包无法表达非 Python 依赖项。 使用转换器库，您通常会依赖诸如 CUDA 之类的东西来支持 GPU，但用户需要知道单独安装它。
  • 转换器库还需要下载千兆字节的模型权重才能做任何有用的事情。 这些被缓存在本地目录中，因此无需在每次运行时都下载它们，但对于某些上下文，您可能希望将这些模型权重作为部署的固定部分。
  • 最后，在发布 pip 或 conda 包时，用户希望您相对灵活地指定依赖项。 例如，transformers 包指定“tensorflow>=2.3”，它声明该包适用于任何版本的 tensorflow 2.3 或更高版本。 这意味着，如果 tensorflow 维护者引入错误或破坏向后兼容性，他们可以有效地导致您的包停止工作（欢迎来到依赖地狱）。 灵活的依赖对库很有用，因为这意味着更多的人可以将你的包安装到他们的环境中。 但是对于部署，例如，如果您在公司内部署模型，您并没有利用这种灵活性，您宁愿确定它每次都能正常工作，以及部署的可重复性。

解决这些问题的一种常见方法是构建容器。 您可以将 CUDA 安装到您的容器中，如果您在图像中包含模型权重，只要您正确管理 Docker层，Docker 将确保您在每台机器上只有该数据的一个副本，并且您将能够选择和测试进入容器的特定版本的 tensorflow。

所以我们已经解决了一堆问题，但是我们用它们换来了一个新问题，那就是每个容器都在自己的小世界中运行，我们必须做一些工作来向用户公开我们的 API。 使用库，消费者可以调用 Python 函数，传入一些输入并取回一些输出。 对于容器，我们的模型更像是一个应用程序或服务，因此消费者没有内置方法可以使用一些输入数据“调用”容器并取回一些输出数据。

一种选择是创建命令行界面，但这需要将输入和输出文件显式绑定到容器。 这感觉有点不自然，但我们可以在 dpokidov的 ImageMagick 容器镜像中看到一个例子。 在“使用”部分，作者建议将本地文件夹绑定到容器中，以便将其作为命令行应用程序运行。

对于 Docker 映像，这个问题的传统答案是公开一个基于 HTTP 的 API，这就是 Gravity AI 的容器所做的。 但这意味着将我们的函数（例如classifier() ）转换为服务，这意味着我们需要弄清楚该服务的放置位置。 再次给出传统答案，我们可以将它部署到 Kubernetes 中，并在其前面使用自动缩放器和负载均衡器，例如，如果您有一个需要调用classifier()的恒定流程流，它可以很好地工作。 但是您可能有一个用例，其中每隔几个小时就会从供应商那里显示一些数据，这会触发批处理作业。 在这种情况下，事情会变得有点奇怪。 您可能处于批处理作业正在运行的情况，调用classifier() ，然后必须等待自动缩放器/Kubernetes 找到可以启动服务的空闲机器，而运行批处理作业的机器处于空闲状态。

换句话说，这两种选择（库 vs 服务）都是合理的，但它们都有自己的缺点。

顺便说一句，您可以想象一种通过 Docker 扩展获得两全其美的方法，该扩展允许您发布一个公开 Python API 的容器，以便有人可以调用sentiment = call_container_api(image="huggingface/transformers", "my input text")直接来自他们的 python 代码。 这实际上是对容器的远程过程调用，该容器不作为服务运行，而是为了按需执行功能而启动。 这感觉像是解决依赖地狱的一种非常重量级的方法，但是如果您的库在幕后使用跨平台内存格式（你好Apache Arrow ！），您可以想象做一些有趣的技巧，比如给容器一个只读视图进入调用者的内存空间以减少开销。 这有点难以置信，但有时勾勒出这些想法有助于澄清我们在使用可用的更实用的技术时所做的权衡。

在本地使用容器中的模型

在本文中，我们将解决这个带有容器的批处理作业场景。 具体来说，假设每天早上，供应商都会向我们提供一个相对较大的转储，其中包含每个人在一夜之间在互联网上（Twitter、Reddit、Seeking Alpha 等）所说的关于标准普尔 500 指数公司的所有内容。 我们希望将这些文本片段输入FinBERT ，这是BERT的一个版本，已针对金融情绪分析进行了微调。 我们将使用 Gravity AI 的FinBERT 容器，但我们还将假设我们在主要面向批处理的环境中运行，因此我们没有设置例如 Kubernetes 集群，即使我们这样做了也可能是由于我们的使用模式，很难正确进行自动缩放。

根据Gravity AI 的文档，如果我们只是在本地机器上进行尝试，使用起来非常简单：

 docker load -i Sentiment_Analysis_o_77f77f.docker.tar.gz docker run -d -p 7000:80 gx-images:t-39c447b9e5b94d7ab75060d0a927807f

然后我们可以写一点胶水代码：

 import time import requests def upload_license_file(base_url: str) -> None: with open("Sentiment Analysis on Financial Text.gravity-ai.key", "r") as f: response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/license/file", files={"License": f}) response.raise_for_status() def call_finbert_container(base_url: str, input_text: str) -> str: add_job_response = requests.post( f"{base_url}/data/add-job", files={ "CallbackUrl": (None, ""), "File": ("temp.txt", input_text, "text/plain"), "MimeType": (None, "text/plain"), } ) add_job_response.raise_for_status() add_job_response_json = add_job_response.json() if ( add_job_response_json.get("isError", False) or add_job_response_json.get("errorMessage") is not None ): raise ValueError(f"Error from server: {add_job_response_json.get('errorMessage')}") job_id = add_job_response_json["data"]["id"] job_status = add_job_response_json["data"]["status"] while job_status != "Complete": status_response = requests.get(f"{base_url}/data/status/{job_id}") status_response.raise_for_status() job_status = status_response.json()["data"]["status"] time.sleep(1) result_response = requests.get(f"{base_url}/data/result/{job_id}") return result_response.text def process_data(): base_url = "http://localhost:7000" upload_license_file(base_url) # Pretend to query input data from somewhere sample_data = [ "Finnish media group Talentum has issued a profit warning", "The loss for the third quarter of 2007 was EUR 0.3 mn smaller than the loss of" " the second quarter of 2007" ] results = [call_finbert_container(base_url, line) for line in sample_data] # Pretend to store the output data somewhere print(results) if __name__ == "__main__": process_data()

我们还假设您已将 Gravity AI 密钥作为Sentiment Analysis on Financial Text.gravity-ai.key下载到当前目录。

在云上使用容器中的模型

这非常适合在本地使用我们的模型，但有时我们可能希望在云上运行它，以访问额外的计算，无论是 CPU 还是 GPU，或者使用例如 Airflow 将其作为计划作业运行。 通常要做的是将我们的代码（finbert_local_example.py 及其依赖项）打包为一个容器，这意味着现在我们有两个容器——一个包含我们的胶水代码，另一个是我们需要一起启动和协调的 FinBERT 容器（即我们的胶水代码容器需要知道 FinBERT 容器的地址/名称才能访问它）。 我们可能会开始接触Docker Compose ，它非常适合长时间运行的服务，但在临时分布式批处理作业或计划作业的上下文中，使用它会很棘手。

相反，我们将使用Meadowrun来完成大部分繁重的工作。 要继续进行，您需要设置一个环境。 我们将展示如何在 Windows 上使用 pip，但您应该能够按照您选择的包管理器进行操作（conda 环境不能跨平台工作，因此 conda 只有在 Linux 上才能工作）。

 python -m venv venv venv\Scripts\activate.bat pip install requests meadowrun meadowrun-manage-ec2 install

这将创建一个新的 virtaulenv，添加请求和 meadowrun，然后将 Meadowrun 安装到您的 AWS 账户中。

当您从 Gravity AI 下载容器时，它以 .tar.gz 文件的形式出现，需要上传到容器注册表才能工作。 您将在第一个命令的输出中看到您的帐户 ID，即registryId 。

在我们可以运行一些代码之前还需要一步：我们需要授予 Meadowrun 角色对此 ECR 存储库的权限。

 meadowrun-manage-ec2 grant-permission-to-ecr-repo mygravityai

现在我们可以运行我们的代码：

 import asyncio import meadowrun from finbert_local_example import upload_license_file , call_finbert_container # Normally this would live in S3 or a database somewhere _SAMPLE_DATA = { "Company1" : [ "Commission income fell to EUR 4.6 mn from EUR 5.1 mn in the corresponding " "period in 2007" , "The purchase price will be paid in cash upon the closure of the transaction, " "scheduled for April 1 , 2009" ], "Company2" : [ "The loss for the third quarter of 2007 was EUR 0.3 mn smaller than the loss of" " the second quarter of 2007" , "Consolidated operating profit excluding one-off items was EUR 30.6 mn, up from" " EUR 29.6 mn a year earlier" ] } def process_one_company ( company_name : str ): base_url = "http://container-service-0" data_for_company = _SAMPLE_DATA [ company_name ] upload_license_file ( "http://container-service-0" ) results = [ call_finbert_container ( base_url , line ) for line in data_for_company ] return results async def process_data (): results = await meadowrun . run_map ( process_one_company , [ "Company1" , "Company2" ], meadowrun . AllocCloudInstance ( "EC2" ), meadowrun . Resources ( logical_cpu = 1 , memory_gb = 1.5 , max_eviction_rate = 80 ), await meadowrun . Deployment . mirror_local ( working_directory_globs = [ "*.key" ]), sidecar_containers = meadowrun . ContainerInterpreter ( "012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai" , "finbert" ), ) print ( results ) if __name__ == "__main__" : asyncio . run ( process_data ())

让我们逐行浏览process_data函数。

  • 在这种情况下，我们提供了两个参数（ ["Company1", "Company2"] ），因此我们将并行运行这两个任务。 我们的想法是我们正在拆分工作量，以便我们可以快速完成工作。
  • 在这种情况下，我们为每个任务请求 1 个 CPU 和 1.5 GB 的 RAM。 我们还指定我们可以处理高达 80% 驱逐率（也就是中断概率）的现场实例。
  • 最后，container_services 告诉 Meadowrun 为我们并行运行的每个任务启动一个带有指定镜像的容器。 每个任务都可以作为container-service-0访问其关联的容器，您可以在process_one_company的代码中看到它。 如果您继续进行操作，则需要再次编辑帐户 ID 以匹配您的帐户 ID。

让我们看一下输出中一些更重要的行：

 Launched 1 new instance(s) (total $0.0209/hr) for the remaining 2 workers: ec2-13-59-48-22.us-east-2.compute.amazonaws.com: r5d.large (2.0 CPU, 16.0 GB), spot ($0.0209/hr, 2.5% chance of interruption), will run 2 workers

在这种情况下，我们每小时只需支付 2 美分！

接下来，Meadowrun 将通过构建一个新的容器镜像在 EC2 实例上复制我们的本地环境，然后还拉取我们指定的 FinBERT 容器：

 Building python environment in container 4e4e2c... ... Pulling docker image 012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai:finbert

我们的最终结果将是 FinBERT 模型的一些输出，如下所示：

 [['sentence,logit,prediction,sentiment_score\nCommission income fell to EUR 4.6 mn from EUR 5.1 mn in the corresponding period in 2007,[0.24862055 0.44351986 0.30785954],negative,-0.1948993\n', ...

闭幕致辞

让这些容器自然地用于批处理作业需要一些工作，但 Meadowrun 让它变得简单！



原文:

GravityAI is a marketplace for ML models where data scientists can publish their models as containers, and consumers can subscribe to access those models. In this article, we’ll talk about different options for deploying these containers and then walk through using them for batch jobs via Meadowrun, which is an open-source library for running your Python code and containers in the cloud.

Containers vs libraries

This section lays out some motivation for this post — if you’re interested in just getting things working, please go to the next section!

If you think of an ML model as a library, then it might seem more natural to publish it as a package, either on PyPI for use with pip or Anaconda.org for use with conda, rather than a container. Hugging Face’s transformers is a good example — you run pip install transformers, then your Python interpreter can do things like:

from transformers import pipeline
classifier = pipeline("sentiment-analysis")
classifier("We are very happy to show you the 🤗 Transformers library.")

There are a few disadvantages with this approach:

  • For pip specifically (not conda), there’s no way for packages to express non-Python dependencies. With the transformers library, you’ll usually depend on something like CUDA for GPU support, but users need to know to install that separately.
  • The transformers library also needs to download gigabytes of model weights in order to do anything useful. These get cached in a local directory so they don’t need to be downloaded every time you run, but for some contexts you might want to make these model weights a fixed part of the deployment.
  • Finally, when publishing a pip or conda package, users expect you to specify your dependencies relatively flexibly. E.g. the transformers package specifies “tensorflow>=2.3” which declares that the package works with any version of tensorflow 2.3 or higher. This means that if the tensorflow maintainers introduce a bug or break backwards compatibility, they can effectively cause your package to stop working (welcome to dependency hell). Flexible dependencies are useful for libraries, because it means more people can install your package into their environments. But for deployments, e.g. if you’re deploying a model within your company, you’re not taking advantage of that flexibility and you’d rather have the certainty it’s going to work every time, and reproducibility of the deployment.

One common way to solve these problems is to build a container. You can install CUDA into your container, if you include your model weights in the image Docker will make sure you only have a single copy of that data on each machine as long as you’re managing your Docker layers correctly, and you’ll be able to choose and test the specific version of tensorflow that goes into the container.

So we’ve solved a bunch of problems, but we’ve traded them for a new problem, which is that each container runs in its own little world and we have to do some work to expose our API to our user. With a library, the consumer can just call a Python function, pass in some inputs and get back some outputs. With a container, our model is more like an app or service, so there’s no built-in way for a consumer to “call” the container with some input data and get back some output data.

One option is to create a command line interface, but that requires explicitly binding input and output files to the container. This feels a bit unnatural, but we can see an example of it in this container image of ImageMagick by dpokidov. In the “Usage” section, the author recommends binding a local folder into the container in order to run it as a command line app.

The traditional answer to this question for Docker images is to expose an HTTP-based API, which is what Gravity AI’s containers do. But this means turning our function (e.g. classifier()) into a service, which means we need to figure out where to put this service. To give the traditional answer again, we could deploy it to Kubernetes with an autoscaler and a load balancer in front of it, which can work well if e.g. you have a constant stream of processes that need to call classifier(). But you might instead have a use case where some data shows up from a vendor every few hours which triggers a batch processing job. In that case, things can get a bit weird. You might be in a situation where the batch job is running, calls classifier(), then has to wait for the autoscaler/Kubernetes to find a free machine that can spin up the service while the machine running the batch job is sitting idle.

In other words, both of these options (library vs service) are reasonable, but they come with their own disadvantages.

As a bit of an aside, you could imagine a way to get the best of both worlds with an extension to Docker that would allow you to publish a container that exposes a Python API, so that someone could call sentiment = call_container_api(image="huggingface/transformers", "my input text") directly from their python code. This would effectively be a remote procedure call into a container that is not running as a service but instead spun up just for the purpose of executing a function on-demand. This feels like a really heavyweight approach to solving dependency hell, but if your libraries are using a cross-platform memory format (hello Apache Arrow!) under the covers, you could imagine doing some fun tricks like giving the container a read-only view into the caller’s memory space to reduce the overhead. It’s a bit implausible, but sometimes it’s helpful to sketch out these ideas to clarify the tradeoffs we’re making with the more practical bits of technology we have available.

Using models-in-containers locally

In this article, we’ll tackle this batch jobs-with-containers scenario. To make this concrete, let’s say that every morning, a vendor gives us a relatively large dump of everything everyone has said on the internet (Twitter, Reddit, Seeking Alpha, etc.) about the companies in the S&P 500 overnight. We want to feed these pieces of text into FinBERT, which is a version of BERT that has been fine-tuned for financial sentiment analysis. BERT is a language model from Google that was state of the art when it was published in 2018.

We’ll be using Gravity AI’s container for FinBERT, but we’ll also assume that we operate in a primarily batch process-oriented environment, so we don’t have e.g. a Kubernetes cluster set up and even if we did it would probably be tricky to get the autoscaling right because of our usage pattern.

If we’re just trying this out on our local machine, it’s pretty straightforward to use, as per Gravity AI’s documentation:

docker load -i Sentiment_Analysis_o_77f77f.docker.tar.gz
docker run -d -p 7000:80 gx-images:t-39c447b9e5b94d7ab75060d0a927807f

Sentiment_Analysis_o_77f77f.docker.tar.gz is the name of the file you download from Gravity AI and gx-images:t-39c447b9e5b94d7ab75060d0a927807f is the name of the Docker image once it’s loaded from the .tar.gz file, which will show up in the output of the first command.

And then we can write a bit of glue code:

import time

import requests


def upload_license_file(base_url: str) -> None:
    with open("Sentiment Analysis on Financial Text.gravity-ai.key", "r") as f:
        response = requests.post(f"{base_url}/api/license/file", files={"License": f})
        response.raise_for_status()


def call_finbert_container(base_url: str, input_text: str) -> str:
    add_job_response = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/data/add-job",
        files={
            "CallbackUrl": (None, ""),
            "File": ("temp.txt", input_text, "text/plain"),
            "MimeType": (None, "text/plain"),
        }
    )
    add_job_response.raise_for_status()
    add_job_response_json = add_job_response.json()
    if (
        add_job_response_json.get("isError", False)
        or add_job_response_json.get("errorMessage") is not None
    ):
        raise ValueError(f"Error from server: {add_job_response_json.get('errorMessage')}")
    job_id = add_job_response_json["data"]["id"]
    job_status = add_job_response_json["data"]["status"]

    while job_status != "Complete":
        status_response = requests.get(f"{base_url}/data/status/{job_id}")
        status_response.raise_for_status()
        job_status = status_response.json()["data"]["status"]
        time.sleep(1)

    result_response = requests.get(f"{base_url}/data/result/{job_id}")
    return result_response.text


def process_data():
    base_url = "http://localhost:7000"

    upload_license_file(base_url)

    # Pretend to query input data from somewhere
    sample_data = [
        "Finnish media group Talentum has issued a profit warning",
        "The loss for the third quarter of 2007 was EUR 0.3 mn smaller than the loss of"
        " the second quarter of 2007"
    ]

    results = [call_finbert_container(base_url, line) for line in sample_data]

    # Pretend to store the output data somewhere
    print(results)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    process_data()

call_finbert_container calls the REST API provided by the container to submit a job, polls for the job completion, and then returns the job result. process_data pretends to get some text data and processes it using our container, and then pretends to write the output somewhere else. We’re also assuming you’ve downloaded the Gravity AI key to the current directory as Sentiment Analysis on Financial Text.gravity-ai.key.

Using models-in-containers on the cloud

This works great for playing around with our model locally, but at some point we’ll probably want to run this on the cloud, either to access additional compute whether that’s CPU or GPU, or to run this as a scheduled job with e.g. Airflow. The usual thing to do is package up our code (finbert_local_example.py and its dependencies) as a container, which means now we have two containers — one containing our glue code, and the FinBERT container that we need to launch together and coordinate (i.e. our glue code container needs to know the address/name of the FinBERT container to access it). We might start reaching for Docker Compose which works great for long-running services, but in the context of an ad-hoc distributed batch job or a scheduled job, it will be tricky to work with.

Instead, we’ll use Meadowrun to do most of the heavy lifting. Meadowrun will not only take care of the usual difficulties of allocating instances, deploying our code, etc., but also help launch an extra container and make it available to our code.

To follow along, you’ll need to set up an environment. We’ll show how this works with pip on Windows, but you should be able to follow along the package manager of your choice (conda environments don’t work across platforms, so conda will only work if you’re on Linux).

python -m venv venv
venv\Scripts\activate.bat
pip install requests meadowrun
meadowrun-manage-ec2 install

This creates a new virtaulenv, adds requests and meadowrun, and then installs Meadowrun into your AWS account.

When you download a container from Gravity AI, it comes as a .tar.gz file that needs to get uploaded to a container registry in order to work. There are slightly longer instructions in the Gravity AI documentation, but here’s a short version of how to create an ECR (Elastic Container Registry) repository then upload a container from Gravity AI to it:

aws ecr create-repository --repository-name mygravityaiaws ecr get-login-password | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.comdocker tag gx-images:t-39c447b9e5b94d7ab75060d0a927807f 012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai:finbertdocker push 012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai:finbert

012345678901 appears in a few places in this snippet and it needs to be replaced with your account id. You’ll see your account id in the output of the first command as registryId.

One more step before we can run some code: we’ll need to give the Meadowrun role permissions to this ECR repository.

meadowrun-manage-ec2 grant-permission-to-ecr-repo mygravityai

Now we can run our code:

import asyncio import meadowrun from finbert_local_example import upload_license_file, call_finbert_container # Normally this would live in S3 or a database somewhere
_SAMPLE_DATA = { "Company1": [ "Commission income fell to EUR 4.6 mn from EUR 5.1 mn in the corresponding " "period in 2007", "The purchase price will be paid in cash upon the closure of the transaction, " "scheduled for April 1 , 2009" ], "Company2": [ "The loss for the third quarter of 2007 was EUR 0.3 mn smaller than the loss of" " the second quarter of 2007", "Consolidated operating profit excluding one-off items was EUR 30.6 mn, up from" " EUR 29.6 mn a year earlier" ]
} def process_one_company(company_name: str): base_url = "http://container-service-0" data_for_company = _SAMPLE_DATA[company_name] upload_license_file("http://container-service-0") results = [call_finbert_container(base_url, line) for line in data_for_company] return results async def process_data(): results = await meadowrun.run_map( process_one_company, ["Company1", "Company2"], meadowrun.AllocCloudInstance("EC2"), meadowrun.Resources(logical_cpu=1, memory_gb=1.5, max_eviction_rate=80), await meadowrun.Deployment.mirror_local(working_directory_globs=["*.key"]), sidecar_containers=meadowrun.ContainerInterpreter( "012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai", "finbert" ), ) print(results) if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(process_data())

Let’s walk through the process_data function line by line.

  • Meadowrun’s run_map function runs the specified function (in this case process_one_company) in parallel on the cloud. In this case, we provide two arguments (["Company1", "Company2"]) so we’ll run these two tasks in parallel. The idea is that we’re splitting up the workload so that we can finish the job quickly.
  • AllocCloudInstance tells Meadowrun to launch an EC2 instance to run this job if we don’t have one running already and Resources tells Meadowrun what resources are needed to run this code. In this case we’re requesting 1 CPU and 1.5 GB of RAM per task. We’re also specifying that we we’re okay with spot instances up to an 80% eviction rate (aka probability of interruption).
  • mirror_local tells Meadowrun that we want to use the code in the current directory, which is important as we’re reusing some code from finbert_local_example.py. Meadowrun only uploads .py files by default, but in our case, we need to include the .key file in our current working directory so that we can apply the Gravity AI license.
  • Finally, container_services tells Meadowrun to launch a container with the specified image for every task we have running in parallel. Each task can access its associated container as container-service-0, which you can see in the code for process_one_company. If you’re following along, you’ll need to edit the account id again to match your account id.

Let’s look at a few of the more important lines from the output:

Launched 1 new instance(s) (total $0.0209/hr) for the remaining 2 workers:
 ec2-13-59-48-22.us-east-2.compute.amazonaws.com: r5d.large (2.0 CPU, 16.0 GB), spot ($0.0209/hr, 2.5% chance of interruption), will run 2 workers

Here Meadowrun is telling us that it’s launching the cheapest EC2 instance that can run our job. In this case, we’re only paying 2¢ per hour!

Next, Meadowrun will replicate our local environment on the EC2 instance by building a new container image, and then also pull the FinBERT container that we specified:

Building python environment in container  4e4e2c...
...
Pulling docker image 012345678901.dkr.ecr.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/mygravityai:finbert

Our final result will be some output from the FinBERT model that looks like:

[['sentence,logit,prediction,sentiment_score\nCommission income fell to EUR 4.6 mn from EUR 5.1 mn in the corresponding period in 2007,[0.24862055 0.44351986 0.30785954],negative,-0.1948993\n',
...

Closing remarks

Gravity AI packages up ML models as containers which are really easy to use as services. Making these containers work naturally for batch jobs takes a bit of work, but Meadowrun makes it easy!

item.title: 你确定你的备份有效吗？ | Are you sure your backups work?
item.description:
希望每个人，我都有我所有数据的备份。 初始设置后，我通常会忘记它，直到出现问题，我需要恢复我的数据。 这篇文章只是一个提醒：请检查您是否可以恢复您的数据。

但是如果你想知道我的设置

我选择了kopia.io 。 它有一个漂亮的图形（和终端）界面，支持云和本地存储、加密、压缩，就目前而言：它可以工作。
在此之前，我使用：

  • 中村健一郎—— 一旦暂停，finloop 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，finloop 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，finloop 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 finloop 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 finloop 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 finloop 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

As hopefully everyone, I have backups of all my data. After initial setup, I usually forget about it until something breaks, and I need to restore my data. This post is just a reminder: please check if you can restore your data.

But if you want to know my setup

I settled with kopia.io. It has a nice graphical (and terminal) interface, support for cloud and local storage, encryption, compression and as for now: it just works.
Before that, I used:

  • Duplicati: buggy, my backups broke after few months (problem with cloud connection)
  • Restic: no compression is a dealbreaker for me, as this increases storage costs in a cloud like Backblaze B2

Kenichiro Nakamura - Jul 27

item.title: 使用 Angular 和容器开始复杂的投资组合之旅 | Starting a journey of a complicated Portfolio with Angular and Containers
item.description:
古斯塔沃·卡贝萨尔

发表于所以这就是简单的故事，这将是一个连续的系列，我想更新我用 hugo 制作的旧作品集。 所以我说“为什么不”并删除了旧信息，这对我来说很糟糕，因为 repo 在旧文件中而不是在线，所以我也丢失了它。 我决定从头开始，更好地了解并测试我想做的新技术，因此我开始在一个简单的网站上工作，我想使用 Angular 来看看我如何实现新想法，即使它是一个基本不是那种动态的网站。

工具

对于工具，我将使用：

网站

  • 角
  • 尾风css
  • 笑话
  • 柏
  • 故事书

开发运维

  • 詹金斯
  • 依赖机器人
  • 云润
  • 码头工人中心
  • 构建包。

送货

理念

我将在 Angular 上创建一个网站，使用 jenkins 在每个分支上进行测试，在拉取请求时，我将创建一个容器，将其存储在 docker hub 上并将其部署在 cloudrun 上。

简单 是的，让我们看看它是怎么回事。

一旦暂停，meisterveda 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

如果 meisterveda 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 meisterveda 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Gustavo Cabezal

Posted on Aug 17

So here is the story in a few words, it will be a continuous series, I wanted to update an old portfolio that I had made with hugo. So I said "why not" and deleted the old information, bad for me that the repo was in old files and not online and so I lost that too. I decided to start it all over and get to know better and test new technologies that I wanted to do, and thus I got working on a simple site, I wanted to use angular to see how I can implement new ideas, even if its a basic not that dynamic site.

The tools

For tooling I will be working with:

Website

  • Angular
  • Tailwindcss
  • Jest
  • Cypress
  • Storybooks

DevOps

  • Jenkins
  • Dependabot
  • CloudRun
  • Docker Hub
  • Buildpacks.

Delivery

The Idea

I will be making a website on angular, having testing on each branch with jenkins, on pull requests I will be creating a container, storing it on docker hub and deploying it on cloudrun.

Simple Yes, lets see how it goes.

item.title: CSS颜色技巧，让你的生活更轻松 | CSS color tricks to make your life easier
item.description:
赛卡特比沙尔

发表于但是有一些开发人员不知道的技巧，可以让您在使用 CSS 颜色时更轻松。

继续阅读以了解它们。

您不需要逗号分隔值

最近 CSS 的语法已针对 rgb 和 hsl 进行了更新，因为我们在不久的将来会有许多新的语法（函数）。 我们可以处理更大的色域。

现在我们在指定 rgba 值时不必添加逗号。 例子 ： 

 .color-old-syntax { background-color : rgb(100,255,120); } .color-new-syntax { background-color : rgb(100 255 120); }

您不需要a in 来指定不透明度

虽然我们需要指定rgba或hsla以便我们可以向元素添加不透明度值，但在新语法中我们不需要它。 例子 ：

 .color-old-syntax { background-color : hsla(100,50,50,0.5) } .color-new-syntax { background-color : hsl(100 50 50 / 0.5) }

请注意，您仍然可以在同一函数中指定不透明度。 语法已从逗号分隔值更改为正斜杠（仅用于指定不透明度值）。

用 rgb 中的百分比指定值

您现在可以以百分比指定颜色值，而不是在 0 到 255 的范围内指定它们。

 .color-old-syntax { background-color : rgb(127,100,100,0.5) } .color-new-syntax { background-color : rgb(50%,40%,40%,0.5) }

这是一种更好的方式来可视化颜色在以特定百分比混合时的表现或比较两种 rgb 颜色。
不用说，如果您不想使用，则不必使用它。 一切都是向后兼容的。

此外，您还可以在 rgb() 函数中混合和匹配百分比和绝对值。 但我不建议这样做。 例子 ：

 .rgb-background-new { background-color : rgb(100% 255 50%) } //is same as .rgb-background-old { background-color : rgb(255 255 127) }

这种混合和匹配功能现在只是在 chrome 中。

色调是您想要获得的实际颜色。 它在 0 到 360 度的范围内，其中 0 和 360 是纯红色。 饱和度是您想要某种色调的颜色量。 这是以百分比指定的。

颜色的亮度可以描述为**你想给颜色多少光**。 它也是百分比，其中 0% 为黑色，100% 为白色。 如果您想获得最纯正的颜色，50% 是最佳选择。


 .hsl-map-one { background-color: hsl(0 50% 50%); } // is same as .hsl-map-two { background-color : hsl(360 50% 50%); }

红色 -- 0 绿色 -- 120 蓝色 -- 240

如果您需要在三基色之间指定颜色（例如，红色和绿色混合的任何颜色都在 0 到 120 之间），这种方式的 hsl 更直观，您只需更改函数的第一个值与 rgb() 不同。

我们知道 hsl() 色调适用于色轮的概念，色调值可以超过 360 度是有道理的。示例：

 .hue-value-one { background-color : hsl(120 50% 50%) } // is same as .hue-value-one { background-color : hsl(480 50% 50%) }

色调值也可以为负值。 因此 hsl(120 50% 50%) 与 hsl(-240 50 50) 相同。

当您使用 javascript 动态更改颜色值时，这些属性特别有用。 您不必在 javaScript 上使用大量计算来完成这项工作。 很方便不是吗？

不透明度现在可以按百分比工作

并不是说它会有很大的不同，但最好知道我们现在可以用十进制和百分比指定不透明度值。

 .opacity-box-one { background-color : rgb (100 200 100 / 0.5); } // is same as .opacity-box-two { background-color : rgb (100 200 100 / 50%); }

这就对了。 如果您知道任何其他技巧或更新的 css 功能，请随时在下面发表评论。 这对所有的读者和我都非常有帮助。

卡兰·普拉塔普·辛格—— 一旦暂停，saikatbishal 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，saikatbishal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，saikatbishal 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 saikatbishal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 saikatbishal：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 saikatbishal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

SAIKAT BISHAL

Posted on Aug 17

Colors in CSS are pretty easy to handle and do not pose much difficulty. But there are certain tricks not many developers know, that could make your life much easier while working with CSS colors.

Read on to learn about them.

You do not need comma separated values

Very recently the syntax of CSS has been updated for rgb and hsl because we have many new syntaxes(functions) coming in the near future. We can work with bigger color gamuts.

Now we do not have to add commas when specifying rgba values. Example :

.color-old-syntax {
background-color : rgb(100,255,120);

}

.color-new-syntax {
background-color : rgb(100 255 120);
}

You do not need an a in to specify opacity

While we needed to specify rgba or hsla so that we could add an opacity value to the element, we do not need it in the new syntax. Example :

.color-old-syntax {
background-color : hsla(100,50,50,0.5)
}
.color-new-syntax {
background-color : hsl(100 50 50 / 0.5)
}

Note that you still would be able to specify the opacity in the same function. The syntax has changed from comma separated values to a forward slash (just to specify the opacity value).

Specify values with percentages in rgb

You can now specify the color values in percentages rather than specifying them on a 0 to 255 scale.

.color-old-syntax {
background-color : rgb(127,100,100,0.5)
}
.color-new-syntax {
background-color : rgb(50%,40%,40%,0.5)
}

This is a better way to visualise how the colors would behave when mixed up in certain percentages or to compare two rgb colors.
Needless to say, you do not have to use this if you do not want to. Everything is backward compatible.

What's more, you could also mix and match percentage and absolute values inside the rgb() function. But I would not recommend that. Example :


.rgb-background-new {
background-color : rgb(100% 255 50%)
}

//is same as

.rgb-background-old {
background-color : rgb(255 255 127)

}

This mix and match feature is just in chrome right now.

hsl() is more intuitive than rgb()

hsl stands for hue saturation lightness
Hue is the actual color you want to get. It is in the range of 0 to 360 degree where 0 and 360 is pure red. (see the hsl color wheel for more info).
Saturation is the amount of color that you want of a certain hue. This is specified in percentages.

The lightness of a color can be described as ** how much light** you want to give the color. It is also in percentage where 0% is black and 100% is white. 50% is the sweet spot if you want to get the most pure color.


.hsl-map-one {
background-color: hsl(0 50% 50%);
}
// is same as
.hsl-map-two {
background-color : hsl(360 50% 50%); 
}

red -- 0 green -- 120 blue -- 240

hsl in this way is more intuitive if you need to specify colors in between the three primary color (for example anything that is a mix of red and green would be between 0 and 120), you just need to change the first value of the function for that unlike rgb().

Hue values in hsl() can go beyond 0 degree and 360 degree

As we know hsl() hue works on the concept of color wheel, it makes sense that the hue value can go beyond 360. Example :

.hue-value-one {
background-color : hsl(120 50% 50%)
}

// is same as  

.hue-value-one {
background-color : hsl(480 50% 50%)
}

The hue value can also go negative. (You can visualise this as the values go from 0 to negative, the arrow rotates in counterclock wise direction). Hence hsl(120 50% 50%) is same as hsl(-240 50 50).

These properties are particularly useful when you use javascript to dynamically change the color values. You do not have to use a lot of calculation on javaScript to make this work. Quite handy isn't it?

Opacity can now work in percentages

Not that it would make much difference, yet it is better to know that we can now specify the opacity values in decimal as well as in percentages.

.opacity-box-one {
background-color : rgb (100  200 100 / 0.5);
}

// is same as

.opacity-box-two {
background-color : rgb (100 200 100 / 50%);
}

That is it. If you know any other tricks or newer css features , feel free to comment that below. It would be very helpful for all the readers and me as well.

Karan Pratap Singh - Aug 1

Once unsuspended, saikatbishal will be able to comment and publish posts again.

item.title: [React] 使用 Apexcharts 实时更新数据 - JSON API | [React] Real time updates data using Apexcharts - JSON API
item.description:
德里克兹r_

发表于许多交易网站（如果不是全部）使用动画图表显示许多用例的实时数据。 在本教程中，我将带您了解如何在您的 React 项目中实现相同的功能。

让我们直接跳进去。

先决条件

  • 对Reactjs, React hook, TailwindCSS,和JSON API有基本的了解。

  • 单击此处GitHub以在本地计算机中克隆存储库。 演示


    如果您没有克隆 repo，请检查先决条件中的链接。

    在我们的server directory中，我们有一个名为data.json的文件，它将成为我们的假数据库。 如您所见，它有一个空的 json 对象。

    稍后我们将在那里添加数据。

客户部分
在web-client directory中，在 src 我们有 2 个文件夹 Chart 和 FiciGame
在 FiciGame 中，我们还有两个文件， UpdateFici.js和index.jsx ，在 Chart 中我们有index.jsx在 UpdateFici.js 中，我们将创建一个将随机数据发送到我们的 JSON 服务器的函数。

 import moment from ' moment ' ; export const sendRandomData = async () => { const currentDate = new Date (); const randNumber = Math . floor ( Math . random () * 100 ); const showDate = moment ( currentDate ). format ( ' HH:mm:ss ' ); const doc = { time : showDate , bid : randNumber , } await fetch ( ' http://localhost:7000/data ' , { method : ' POST ' , body : JSON . stringify ( doc ), headers : { ' Content-Type ' : ' application/json ' } }) }

如您所见，我们正在发送一个带有time和randomNumber的对象，稍后将在 Chart 组件中使用它。

接下来，在index.jsx中，我们将导入sendRandomData并创建两个函数。 第一个是在setIntervals的帮助下每 2 秒（以毫秒 => 2000 为单位）在 useEffect 中调用一个 fetch 函数。

 import { useEffect , useState } from " react " ; import { sendRandomData } from " ./UpdateFici " ; // Fetch data from the fake API const fetchFici = async () => { const response = await fetch ( ' http://localhost:7000/data ' ) console . log ( response ); response . json (). then ( data => { setBidData ( data ) }) } // Call the fetchFici function every 2 second useEffect (() => { const timer = setInterval (() => { fetchFici () }, 2000 ); return () => { clearInterval ( timer ) } }, [])

第二个，每 4 秒，将调用我们早期创建的sendRandomData函数。

 useEffect (() => { const timer2 = setInterval (() => { sendRandomData () }, 10000 ); return () => { clearInterval ( timer2 ) } }, [])

接下来，我们需要创建将实时显示数据的图表。 为此，让我们进入 Chart 文件夹中的 index.jsx 并在下面添加以下代码：

 import React from ' react ' import Chart from " react-apexcharts " ; const ApexChart = ({ data }) => { return ( < div > < Chart type = " area " height = { 300 } width = ' 100% ' series = {[ { name : " Commits " , data : data ?. map ( data => data . bid ) } ]} options = {{ chart : { toolbar : { show : false }, }, colors : [ ' #f90000 ' ], stroke : { width : 1 , curve : ' smooth ' }, dataLabels : { enabled : false }, xaxis : { categories : data ?. map ( data => data . time ), }, yaxis : { show : false , } }} / > < /div > ) } export default ApexChart

这个片段代码有什么作用？ 从 react-apexcharts 导入 Chart 组件，然后在我们的 ApexChart 中调用它，我们将在其中传递一个参数，该参数将接收从我们的假 API 获取的数据。 数据返回一个对象（时间和出价）。

然后在 ApexChart 组件中，我们通过数据进行映射，并在数据中传递出价和在类别中传递时间。 最后让我们在 FiciGame 中导入 ApexChart 并传递从我们的假 API 接收到的数据。

 import ApexChart from " ../Chart " ; ... return ( < ApexChart data = { bidData } / > )

如果一切正常，您现在可以刷新页面并检查服务器存储库中的 data.json 文件，您会看到，数据会自动添加。

结果：

这是实时数据模拟更新的完美基础示例。 您可以根据需要在辅助项目中对其进行调整或使其变得更好。

旁注📝

在您的 devTool 中，在网络选项卡中，您将看到每 3 秒发送一次请求，如果您计划使用每秒更新其数据的 API，我建议您阅读有关WebSocket的更多信息。

我们的教程已经结束，我希望这能让您很好地了解如何为使用 apexcharts 的小型项目集成实时数据更新。

此项目的GitHub源代码。

如果您想自定义图表，请查看apexcharts 文档。
任何问题？ 在下面发表评论或在Twitter 上与我聊天

亚历克斯·达米安·内格鲁—— 一旦暂停，derick1530 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，derick1530 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，derick1530 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 derick1530 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 derick1530 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 derick1530 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Derickzr_

Posted on Aug 17

A guide on how to display live data fetched from a fake JSON API using React & react-apexcharts apexchart.

Many trading websites if not all, use an animated chart that displays real-time data for many use cases. In this tutorial, I will take you through how you can implement the same functionality in your React project.

Let's jump right in.

Prerequisite

  • A basic understanding of Reactjs, React hook, TailwindCSS, and JSON API.

  • Click here GitHub to clone the repo in your local machine. (follow the instruction in the .ReadMe file on how to run the application).

DEMO


If you didn't clone the repo, check the link in the prerequisite.

✅We assume that your web app and server are running.

JSON SERVER PART
In our server directory, we have a file named data.json which is going to be our fake database. as you can see it has an empty json object.

We will be adding data there later.

CLIENT PART
In web-client directory, inside src we have 2 folders Chart and FiciGame
Inside FiciGame, we have two more files, UpdateFici.js, and index.jsx and in Chart we have index.jsx Inside UpdateFici.js we are going to create a function that will send random data to our JSON server.

import moment from 'moment'; export const sendRandomData = async () => { const currentDate = new Date(); const randNumber = Math.floor(Math.random() * 100); const showDate = moment(currentDate).format('HH:mm:ss'); const doc = { time: showDate, bid: randNumber, } await fetch('http://localhost:7000/data', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(doc), headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } })
}

As you can see we are sending an object with time and a randomNumber, which will be used in the Chart component later.

Next, inside index.jsx we are going to import sendRandomData and create two functions. The first is going to call a fetch function inside a useEffect every 2 seconds (in millisecond => 2000) with the help of setIntervals.

import {useEffect,useState} from "react";
import { sendRandomData } from "./UpdateFici"; // Fetch data from the fake API const fetchFici = async () => { const response = await fetch('http://localhost:7000/data') console.log(response); response.json().then(data => { setBidData(data) }) } // Call the fetchFici function every 2 second useEffect(() => { const timer = setInterval(() => { fetchFici() }, 2000); return () => { clearInterval(timer) } }, [])

The second, every 4 seconds, will be calling sendRandomData function we created early.

 useEffect(() => { const timer2 = setInterval(() => { sendRandomData() }, 10000); return () => { clearInterval(timer2) } }, [])

Next we need to create the Chart where data will be displayed in real time. To do that let's go in index.jsx in our Chart folder and add this code below:

import React from 'react'
import Chart from "react-apexcharts"; const ApexChart = ({ data }) => { return ( <div> <Chart type="area" height={300} width='100%' series={[ { name: "Commits", data: data?.map(data => data.bid) } ]} options={{ chart: { toolbar: { show: false }, }, colors: ['#f90000'], stroke: { width: 1, curve: 'smooth' }, dataLabels: { enabled: false }, xaxis: { categories: data?.map(data => data.time), }, yaxis: { show: false, } }} />
 </div>
 )
} export default ApexChart

What does this snippet code do? Import the Chart component from react-apexcharts then calling it inside our ApexChart in which we are passing a parameter that is going to receive the data fetched from our fake API. the data returns an object (time and bid).

Then inside ApexChart component, we are mapping through data and passing bids in data and time in categories. (we used the ? to avoid any error in case the data is not available yet).

Last let import ApexChart in our FiciGame and pass the data received from our fake API.

import ApexChart from "../Chart";
... return ( <ApexChart data={bidData} />
 )

if everything works perfectly you can now refresh the page and check data.json file in the server repository, you will see, data get added automatically.

Result:

This is a perfect basic example of a simulation update of real-time data. You can adjust it in your side-project as you like or make it much better.

side note📝

In your devTool, in the network tab, you will see requests are sent every 3 seconds, If you are planning to use an API that updates its data every second, I will suggest you read more about WebSocket.

We've reached the end of our tutorial, I hope this gave you a good idea of how to integrate a real-time data update for your small project that uses apexcharts.

GitHub source code for this project.

if you want to customize your chart check apexcharts documentation.
any question? leave a comment below or chat with me on Twitter

Alex Damian Negru - Aug 8
